title: cytisus procerus
intro: Cytisus procerus, also known as the Scotch broom, is a perennial legume native to the Mediterranean region and Western Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a diuretic and to reduce fever. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. Cytisus procerus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
